```text
    41→    tooltips: {
    42→      to: function(value) {
    43→        const v = 0 - Math.pow(50, 2)/value;
    44→        return Math.round(v) + '%'; 
    45→      }
    46→    },
    47→    range: {
    48→        'min': 25,
    49→        '80%': [200, 100],
    50→        'max': [2500]
    51→    }
    52→}).on('update', (values, handle) => {

    54→  previewPalette();
    55→});
    56→
    57→function previewPalette() {
    58→  document.getElementById('preview').innerHTML = getPaletteHTML(
    59→      generateNeutrals(
    60→        {hue: hue, distance: 0.01, max: 1.01, min: 0.03, saturation: saturation}
    61→      )
    62→  );
    63→  
    64→  document.getElementById('miniPreview').innerHTML = getMiniPaletteHTML(
    65→      generateNeutrals(
    66→        {hue: hue, distance: 0.01, max: 1.01, min: 0.03, saturation: saturation}
    67→      )
    68→  );
    69→}
    70→
    71→/*
    72→  visual representation
    73→  https://www.desmos.com/calculator/02ufrfsuzy
    74→*/
    75→function getSaturation2(l) {
    76→  const o = 50; // ranges goes from 0 to 100, 50 is the lowest value 
    77→  const p = sMod;
    78→  const v = 1 + (Math.pow(l - o, 2)/p - Math.pow(o, 2)/p) / 100;
    79→  return v;
    80→}
    81→
    82→function generateNeutrals(params) {
    83→  let {
    84→    hue = 200,
    85→    saturation = 0.1,
    86→    min = 0.04,
    87→    max = 1,
    88→    distance = 0.02
    89→  } = params || {};
    90→  
    91→  let tokens = {};
    92→  
    93→  let colorsArray = [];
    94→  let value = min;
    95→  let step = 0;
    96→  console.clear()
    97→  while (value < max ) {
    98→    const i = parseInt(value * 100);
    99→    const sK = getSaturation2(i);
   100→
   101→    console.log(sK);
   102→    
   103→    const color = chroma.hsl(hue, saturation * sK, value);
   104→    tokens[`grey-${i}`] = {
   105→      '$value': color.css('hsl')
   106→    }
   107→    
   108→    colorsArray.push(
   109→      color.css('hsl')
   110→    )
   111→    step++;
   112→    value = step * distance + min;
   113→  }
   114→  console.log(tokens);
   115→  return colorsArray.reverse();
   116→}
   117→
   118→function getPaletteHTML(colors) {
   119→  let markup = [];
   120→  colors.forEach((c, i) => {
   121→    
   122→    let contrast = [
   123→      chroma.contrast("white", c),
   124→      chroma.contrast("#1d1d1d", c)
   125→    ];
   126→
   127→    markup.push(`
   128→      <div class="color-box" style="background: ${c}; color: ${contrast[0] < 2.9 ? "#1d1d1d" : "#FFF"}">
   129→        <div class="row">
   130→          <b>grey-${100-i}</b>
   131→        </div>
   132→        <div class="row">
   133→          <span>hsl</span><span>${outputHSL(c)}</span>
   134→        </div>
   135→      </div>
   136→    `);
   137→  });
   138→  
   139→  return markup.join('');
   140→}
   141→
   142→function getMiniPaletteHTML(colors) {
   143→  let markup = [];
   144→  colors.forEach((c, i) => {
   145→    if(true) {
   146→      markup.push(`
   147→        <div class="color-box" style="background: ${c};" title="grey-${i}">
   148→        </div>
   149→      `);      
   150→    }
   151→  });
   152→  
   153→  return markup.join('');
   154→}
   155→
   156→function outputHSL(color) {
   157→  const [h, s, l] = chroma(color).hsl();
   158→  return `${Math.round(h)|| 0}deg ${Math.round(roundTwoDigits(s) * 100)}% ${Math.round(roundTwoDigits(l) * 100)}%`;
   159→}
   160→
   161→function roundTwoDigits(num) {
   162→  return Math.round((num + Number.EPSILON) * 100) / 100;
   163→}
   164→
```

 `./app/src/components/palette/PaletteGrid.tsx`

```text
     1→import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
     2→import { ColorSwatch } from "./ColorSwatch";
     3→import { ToggleSwitch } from "../controls/ToggleSwitch";
     4→import { useCopyToClipboard } from "../../hooks/useCopyToClipboard";
     5→import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";
     6→
     7→interface PaletteGridProps {
     8→  entries: ColorEntry[];
     9→}
    10→
    11→const STEP_OPTIONS = [8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48, 98];
    12→
    13→type Orientation = "picker" | "rotated";
    14→
    15→function sampleEntries(entries: ColorEntry[], count: number): ColorEntry[] {
    16→  if (count >= entries.length) return entries;
    17→  const step = (entries.length - 1) / (count - 1);
    18→  return Array.from({ length: count }, (_, i) =>
    19→    entries[Math.round(i * step)],
    20→  );
    21→}
    22→
    23→// Find the column count that produces no orphans and the most square-ish grid
    24→function bestCols(total: number): number {
    25→  const target = Math.round(Math.sqrt(total));
    26→  // Search outward from the square root for a clean divisor
    27→  for (let d = 0; d <= target; d++) {
    28→    if ((target + d) > 0 && total % (target + d) === 0) return target + d;
    29→    if ((target - d) > 0 && total % (target - d) === 0) return target - d;
    30→  }
    31→  return target;
    32→}
    33→
    34→// Reorder row-major entries into column-major order for CSS grid rendering
    35→function toColumnMajor(entries: ColorEntry[], cols: number): ColorEntry[] {
    36→  const rows = Math.ceil(entries.length / cols);
    37→  const reordered: ColorEntry[] = [];
    38→  for (let r = 0; r < rows; r++) {
    39→    for (let c = 0; c < cols; c++) {
    40→      const idx = c * rows + r;
    41→      if (idx < entries.length) reordered.push(entries[idx]);
    42→    }
    43→  }
    44→  return reordered;
    45→}
    46→
    47→export function PaletteGrid({ entries }: PaletteGridProps) {
    48→  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();
    49→  const [showBorders, setShowBorders] = useState(true);
    50→  const [showLabels, setShowLabels] = useState(true);
    51→  const [swatchCount, setSwatchCount] = useState<number>(entries.length);
    52→  const [orientation, setOrientation] = useState<Orientation>("picker");
    53→
    54→  const { displayEntries, cols } = useMemo(() => {
    55→    const sampled = sampleEntries(entries, swatchCount);
    56→    const cols = bestCols(sampled.length);
    57→
    58→    if (orientation === "picker") {
    59→      // Light at top, dark at bottom — entries are light→dark,
    60→      // row-major: flows left→right across rows, then next row
    61→      return { displayEntries: sampled, cols };
    62→    }
    63→    // Rotated 90°: dark at top-left, light at bottom-right
    64→    // entries reversed to dark→light, then column-major so lightness flows down
    65→    const reversed = sampled.slice().reverse();
    66→    return { displayEntries: toColumnMajor(reversed, cols), cols };
    67→  }, [entries, swatchCount, orientation]);
    68→
    69→  return (
    70→    <div>
    71→      <div className="flex justify-end items-center gap-3 px-4 lg:pr-0 mb-1">
    72→        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
    73→          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/55">swatches</span>
    74→          <select
    75→            value={swatchCount}
    76→            onChange={(e) => setSwatchCount(Number(e.target.value))}
    77→            className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/40 bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer"
    78→          >
    79→            {STEP_OPTIONS.map((n) => (
    80→              <option key={n} value={n} className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">
    81→                {n}
    82→              </option>
    83→            ))}
    84→          </select>
    85→        </label>
    86→        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
    87→          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/55">orientation</span>
    88→          <select
    89→            value={orientation}
    90→            onChange={(e) => setOrientation(e.target.value as Orientation)}
    91→            className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/40 bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer"
    92→          >
    93→            <option value="picker" className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">picker</option>
    94→            <option value="rotated" className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">rotated</option>
    95→          </select>
    96→        </label>
    97→        <ToggleSwitch label="labels" checked={showLabels} onChange={setShowLabels} />
    98→        <ToggleSwitch label="borders" checked={showBorders} onChange={setShowBorders} />
    99→      </div>
   100→      <div
   101→        className={`grid ml-4 mr-4 lg:mr-0 rounded-xl overflow-hidden ${showBorders ? "gap-px bg-white/[0.04]" : "gap-0"}`}
   102→        style={{ gridTemplateColumns: `repeat(${cols}, 1fr)` }}
   103→      >
   104→        {displayEntries.map((entry) => (
   105→          <ColorSwatch
   106→            key={entry.label}
   107→            entry={entry}
   108→            isCopied={copiedLabel === entry.label}
   109→            onCopy={copy}
   110→            showLabels={showLabels}
   111→            compact={swatchCount > 24}
   112→          />
   113→        ))}
   114→      </div>
   115→    </div>
   116→  );
   117→}
   118→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-17 23:59:30Z)**_

I see the issues. On line 51 of `PaletteGrid.tsx`:

```typescript
const [swatchCount, setSwatchCount] = useState<number>(entries.length);
```

The initial state is set to `entries.length` (which is 98), but the dropdown shows "8" because 98 is one of `STEP_OPTIONS` and it's the last one — wait, actually 98 is in the options. Let me re-examine.

The problem is that `useState(entries.length)` initializes `swatchCount` to 98 (the full entries count). The `<select>` shows "8" because 98 happens to match a value in `STEP_OPTIONS`, but wait — the screenshot shows "8" selected. Let me check: `STEP_OPTIONS = [8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48, 98]`. If `swatchCount` is 98, the select should show 98, not 8.

Actually, looking at the screenshot more carefully — it says "swatches 8" with a dropdown. But 98 swatches are displayed. The issue is that `entries.length` is 98 at initialization, so `swatchCount` starts at 98. But the select shows 98 — the user says it shows 8. Let me look again at the screenshot... it does look like it says "8" (the "9" might be cut off or it's truly showing 8).

The real issue: `swatchCount` initializes to `entries.length` (98), which matches the option `98` in the dropdown, so it renders all 98 swatches. But the user likely expects the default to be a smaller number like 8.

Similarly, `showLabels` defaults to `true` but the screenshot shows no labels. Let me check `ColorSwatch`.

```text
     1→import { getContrastColor } from "../../lib/colors";

     5→interface ColorSwatchProps {

     8→  onCopy: (text: string, label: string) => void;
     9→  showLabels?: boolean;
    10→  compact?: boolean;

    13→export function ColorSwatch({ entry, isCopied, onCopy, showLabels = true, compact = false }: ColorSwatchProps) {

    18→    <button

"px-1.5 py-1 min-h-[40px]" : "px-4 py-3 min-h-[72px]"}`}
    21→      style={{ backgroundColor: entry.hex, color: textColor }}

    29→            hsl({entry.hslString})

    31→        </>
    32→      )}

    34→    </button>
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-17 23:59:37Z)**_

Now I see — on line 15: `const showText = showLabels && !compact`. And on line 111 of PaletteGrid: `compact={swatchCount > 24}`. Since `swatchCount` is 98 (> 24), `compact` is `true`, which means `showText` is `false` even though `showLabels` is `true`.

So the two bugs are:
1. `swatchCount` defaults to `entries.length` (98) instead of a reasonable default like 8
2. Because swatchCount is 98 (> 24), labels are hidden via the `compact` prop even though the labels toggle is on

The fix is simple — change the default `swatchCount` to `STEP_OPTIONS[0]` (which is 8):

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-17 23:59:55Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-18 00:00:00Z)**_

The root cause: `swatchCount` was initialized to `entries.length` (98), which meant:
1. All 98 swatches rendered despite the dropdown appearing to show a small number
2. Since 98 > 24, the `compact` prop was `true`, hiding labels even though the "labels" toggle was on

Changed the default to `STEP_OPTIONS[0]` (8), so it now starts with 8 swatches as expected, and labels will be visible since 8 < 24.
